name: srs-documentation
description: Software Requirements Specification documentation following IEEE 830 standard. Use when generating formal SRS documents or compiling gathered requirements into structured documentation.
capabilities: Generate complete SRS documents using IEEE 830 standard structure
Apply validation checklists to verify requirement quality
Create functional and non-functional requirement specifications
Use MoSCoW prioritization for requirement classification
Follow consistent ID conventions for requirements tracking
author: DoubleslashSE
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files with SRS templates and checklists. No executable code, no network access, no file system access beyond standard AI tool permissions, no environment variable access. All 111 static findings are FALSE POSITIVEs caused by pattern matching on documentation markup syntax.
valueStatement: Writing formal Software Requirements Specifications can be time consuming and error prone. This skill provides IEEE 830 compliant templates and checklists to help generate structured, professional SRS documents quickly and consistently.
userTitle: Create formal SRS documents with IEEE 830 templates
limitations: Does not execute code or access external systems
Does not validate requirements against existing codebase
Does not generate test cases or implementation code
Does not integrate with project management tools

useCases.title: Professional SRS documentation
useCases.description: Create formal, standardized Software Requirements Specifications for stakeholder review and development teams.

useCases.title: Requirements quality assurance
useCases.description: Validate gathered requirements against IEEE 830 checklists to ensure completeness and traceability.

useCases.title: Clear requirement specifications
useCases.description: Receive well-structured requirements with proper ID conventions, priority levels, and acceptance criteria.

promptTemplates.title: Basic SRS creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete Software Requirements Specification document using the IEEE 830 standard. Include: 1. Introduction with purpose, scope, definitions, and references. 2. Overall description with product perspective, functions, user characteristics, constraints, and dependencies. 3. Specific requirements with external interfaces, functional requirements, performance requirements, and design constraints. Use {PROJECT_NAME} as the example project.

promptTemplates.title: Functional requirements
promptTemplates.scenario: Add functional requirements to existing SRS
promptTemplates.prompt: Add functional requirements section to my SRS document. For each requirement include: unique ID using FR-XXX format, description using shall for mandatory requirements, priority using MoSCoW method, inputs, processing steps, outputs, error handling, and acceptance criteria. Focus on {FEATURE_AREA} functionality.

promptTemplates.title: NFR specification
promptTemplates.prompt: Create non-functional requirements section for my SRS. Include performance, security, reliability, usability, and maintainability requirements. Each NFR should have measurable criteria with specific targets, priority level, and verification method. Use NFR-XXX-ID format.

promptTemplates.title: Requirements prioritization
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the following requirements and apply MoSCoW prioritization. For each item, assign Must Have, Should Have, Could Have, or Won't Have status. Provide rationale for each prioritization decision. Identify any potential conflicts between requirements and suggest resolutions.

bestPractices: Use shall for mandatory requirements, should for desirable, may for optional
Write requirements that are unambiguous, complete, and verifiable with measurable criteria
Assign unique identifiers to each requirement for traceability throughout the project lifecycle
antiPatterns: Avoid vague terms like fast, user-friendly, or flexible without specific metrics
Do not combine multiple requirements into single statements
Avoid adding implementation details or design constraints in requirements specifications

faq.question: Which AI platforms support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, OpenAI Codex, and Claude Code. Each platform provides Read and Write tools for document creation.

faq.question: What is the maximum size for SRS documents?
faq.answer: There is no fixed limit. The template supports projects of any size by adding additional sections as needed.

faq.question: Can I customize the template for my organization?
faq.answer: Yes. Modify the template sections, ID conventions, or priority systems to match your organization standards.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill runs entirely within your AI session. No data is sent to external servers beyond your AI provider.

faq.question: Why are my requirements marked as incomplete?
faq.answer: Check that each requirement has: unique ID, description with shall, priority, inputs, outputs, and acceptance criteria.

faq.question: How does this compare to other documentation skills?
faq.answer: This skill focuses specifically on IEEE 830 SRS standard with built-in validation. It provides more structured guidance than generic documentation templates.
